title: Telegram image vision with Kimi
goal: Verify that a Kimi-backed Telegram session can inspect an image natively, including visible text when asked.
prompt: Please inspect this image and include any visible text.
prerequisites: KIMI_API_KEY exported in the same shell as the gateway
ESTACODA_TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN exported in the same shell as the gateway
Gateway restarted after the native-vision changes
steps: Send an image containing visible text with the suggested prompt.
Then send a mixed prompt like `Please extract any visible text and summarize what the image is showing.`
Observe whether the reply describes the image directly instead of falling back to metadata-only behavior.
assertions: Bot gives semantic image understanding, not just file metadata.
Simple image/OCR prompt does not needlessly over-select `telegram-media-analysis` on a vision-capable main route.
notes: This is currently live-proven with Kimi specifically, not yet across all providers.
